hyperedges.label: Server-Side Trust Boundary for Pricing & Payments

nodes.label: Daily use — ask questions instead of grepping
nodes.norm_label: daily use — ask questions instead of grepping

nodes.label: CI Verify Job (blocking tests+build, informational lint/typecheck)
nodes.rationale: Wave 2 (OPS-1) gate on every PR/push so a broken build or failing test can't ship unnoticed — the mechanism by which broken-table features and 30 type errors previously reached 'done'. Lint (~70 errors) and typecheck (~24 errors) run with continue-on-error as a pre-existing backlog; flip to blocking once each reaches zero.
nodes.norm_label: ci verify job (blocking tests+build, informational lint/typecheck)

nodes.label: Category A — Database content is English-only
nodes.norm_label: category a — database content is english-only

nodes.label: A1 Sibling _ar Columns Strategy
nodes.rationale: Chosen over JSONB i18n columns (A2), a translations table (A3), and MT-only (A4) because it follows the existing package_routes.name_ar precedent, keeps queries trivial, leaves RLS unchanged, and stays typed by codegen. Trade-off: scales to exactly 2 languages and ~15 columns of schema churn. content_pages uses row-per-locale instead since pages are whole documents.

nodes.label: A4 Machine-Translation Assist for Wizard
nodes.rationale: Kept optional rather than primary storage strategy: zero agency burden and instant catalog coverage, but costs per save and MT quality needs a review flag. Used as a wizard button that pre-fills the Arabic tab, flagged machine-translated until edited.

nodes.label: B1 Server Error Codes → i18n Keys
nodes.rationale: Edge functions return { code, message }; client shows t('serverErrors.' + code) with English message as last-resort fallback. Chosen over server-side translation (B2) because codes are a stable API and locale logic isn't duplicated server-side; B2 also leaves auth errors English.
nodes.norm_label: b1 server error codes → i18n keys

nodes.label: Mapbox Arabic Label Switching (applyMapLanguage)
nodes.rationale: The RTL plugin fixes text shaping, not language; label layers must coalesce name_ar over name on style load, or adopt Mapbox Standard style with language:'ar'.

nodes.label: CRITICAL: Client-Trusted Pricing (total_price writable by client)
nodes.rationale: The INSERT policy validates who is booking, never how much; fix is server-computed totals via SECURITY DEFINER RPC or Edge Function — the same rule Stripe enforces by never trusting client-sent amounts.
nodes.norm_label: critical: client-trusted pricing (total_price writable by client)

nodes.label: CRITICAL: Client-Writable payment_status
nodes.rationale: payment_status must only be written by a trusted server path (payment webhook), never the traveler; traveler UPDATE policy should be scoped to non-financial columns.

nodes.label: HIGH: Review Fraud (no owned/completed booking check)
nodes.rationale: Ratings drive marketplace trust and search ranking; mirror Amazon's verified-purchase model by gating reviews on owned + completed bookings.
nodes.norm_label: high: review fraud (no owned/completed booking check)

nodes.label: HIGH: Unrestricted File Uploads to package-media
nodes.norm_label: high: unrestricted file uploads to package-media

nodes.label: Central Flaw: No Trusted Domain/Service Layer
nodes.rationale: Because there is no backend, rules that must be trusted (price, availability, payment state) have nowhere correct to live — named the central architectural flaw and root cause of the Section 6 security issues. The browser talks straight to Postgres via the anon key, so every guarantee rests on RLS being perfect.
nodes.norm_label: central flaw: no trusted domain/service layer

nodes.label: RLS as the Sole Trust Boundary
nodes.rationale: A legitimate architecture (27 ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY statements, correct has_role() SECURITY DEFINER pattern) — but it means RLS must be perfect, and policy gaps are directly exploitable with only the public anon key.
nodes.norm_label: rls as the sole trust boundary

nodes.label: No Code Splitting — 3.2 MB Single Bundle
nodes.norm_label: no code splitting — 3.2 mb single bundle

nodes.label: SEO Structurally Impossible (client-rendered SPA, no SSR)
nodes.norm_label: seo structurally impossible (client-rendered spa, no ssr)

nodes.label: SEC-1: Agency Self-Verify / Zero Own Commission
nodes.rationale: Root cause: USING-only RLS policy (with_check = null) plus default column grants on is_verified/commission_rate/status/rating. Fixed in Wave 0 via guard trigger + admin/service bypass.
nodes.norm_label: sec-1: agency self-verify / zero own commission

nodes.label: SEC-2: Traveler Rewrites Booking Price/Status/Payment
nodes.rationale: Directly reopens the hole that migration 20260704000100 (edge-function-only inserts) was created to close; corrupts SUM(total_price) revenue stats and unlocks review fraud since create-review only checks status==='completed'. Fixed in Wave 0.

nodes.label: BUS-2: Booking Capacity Not Enforced (guaranteed overbooking)
nodes.rationale: Fixed in Wave 0 with a DB trigger enforcing SUM(participants) per (package_id, booking_date) <= max_participants — an interim guard until a real departures/inventory model replaces the faked availability (BUS-4).
nodes.norm_label: bus-2: booking capacity not enforced (guaranteed overbooking)

nodes.label: DATA-1: travel_agencies→auth.users FK Dropped for Mock Data
nodes.rationale: Schema was mutated to fit demo data. Wave 0 fix converted the 3 unlinked sample agencies into real demo auth accounts and re-attached the FK with ON DELETE CASCADE.
nodes.norm_label: data-1: travel_agencies→auth.users fk dropped for mock data

nodes.label: ARCH-1: Features Query Non-Existent Tables (guides/deals/messages)
nodes.rationale: Owner decision 2026-07-05: Guides = remove (page + hook deleted); Deals and Messages = build real tables + RLS in Wave 4, no longer treated as bugs. tsc --noEmit in CI prevents recurrence of this class.

nodes.label: BUS-3: Review Flow Unreachable (nothing sets status='completed')
nodes.norm_label: bus-3: review flow unreachable (nothing sets status='completed')

nodes.label: Payments Decision: Moyasar (webhook = source of truth)
nodes.rationale: Target market is Saudi/Gulf where mada is a hard requirement; Moyasar supports mada, Apple Pay, Visa/MC, STC Pay, native SAR. Stripe rejected (no mada); Tap Payments is the fallback for wider-Gulf/UAE volume. Golden rule: the provider webhook, verified inside a Supabase Edge Function using the service role, is the sole authority that sets payment_status='paid' — the client is never trusted, which structurally closes SEC-2's payment-tampering vector.
nodes.norm_label: payments decision: moyasar (webhook = source of truth)

nodes.label: Hybrid Supabase + Cloudflare Backend Decision
nodes.rationale: Owner-approved split: Supabase JS client for DB access, Edge Functions for business logic, Postgres RPCs for transactional ops, RLS as the real enforcement layer, Cloudflare R2 + image resizing for storage/delivery, Cloudflare for CDN/DNS/edge security. DB stays on ap-south-1 (~40ms to Gulf) since Cloudflare edge covers site + images — no migration.

nodes.label: Wave 0.5: Storage Migration to Cloudflare R2
nodes.rationale: Migrate pre-launch while data is tiny (~16 files). R2 replaces Supabase Storage package-media; private totravel-media bucket created; Cloudflare image resizing in front of R2 delivers 'fast photos' for Gulf users (also satisfies ARCH-6). Blocked on owner domain + R2 API token.
nodes.norm_label: wave 0.5: storage migration to cloudflare r2

nodes.label: Presigned-Upload Edge Function Gatekeeper (SEC-5/SEC-6 on R2)
nodes.rationale: R2 has no knowledge of Supabase auth, so Supabase Storage RLS protections must be re-implemented as an Edge Function that verifies caller and ownership, validates MIME/size, then issues a short-lived path-scoped upload URL. The finding becomes 'design the gatekeeper correctly' rather than 'patch the policy'; never expose an unrestricted R2 write path.
nodes.norm_label: presigned-upload edge function gatekeeper (sec-5/sec-6 on r2)

nodes.label: OPS-1: No CI/CD, No Automated Gates
nodes.rationale: ARCH-1/2 bugs reached 'done' precisely because nothing prevented a broken build from shipping. Delivered 2026-07-05 as .github/workflows/ci.yml.
nodes.norm_label: ops-1: no ci/cd, no automated gates

nodes.label: OPS-2: TypeScript Strictness Disabled; tsc Errors Ship
nodes.rationale: strict:false + vite build (SWC) never type-checks, so the type system is decorative exactly where Supabase null-handling matters; burn-down in progress (lint 70→30), full strict blocked on Wave-4 Deals/Messages tables.
nodes.norm_label: ops-2: typescript strictness disabled; tsc errors ship

nodes.label: Wave 0: Critical Security & Integrity Blockers (complete)
nodes.rationale: All 8 launch-gate blockers resolved and live-verified 2026-07-05 against project saouviwryaswacjtfswc, every migration mirrored in supabase/migrations. SEC-1/2/4 shipped first because they are one-to-few-line RLS changes closing live-verified exploits and unblock trustworthy revenue reporting.

nodes.label: WIZ-6: Atomic save_package Postgres RPC
nodes.rationale: Replaced the non-atomic, duplicated JS multi-insert save (data-loss risk, ARCH-4) with one Postgres function doing the full multi-table upsert in a single transaction; useCreatePackage and EditPackage share one payload builder, deleting ~240 duplicated lines. Also fixed WIZ-14 (routes silently dropped on create).

nodes.label: WIZ-8: DB Guard Blocks Agency Self-Feature / Self-Publish
nodes.rationale: enforce_package_update_guard trigger blocks non-admin/non-service changes to featured and any status outside draft/pending, enforcing the admin-approval publish flow (submit→pending, admin→published) at the DB level.
nodes.norm_label: wiz-8: db guard blocks agency self-feature / self-publish

nodes.label: Option C: Stay on Managed Supabase
nodes.rationale: Recommended baseline: zero migration (schema, RLS, auth, storage already work), zero ops, and Edge Functions provide the trusted server tier with no new vendor. Start Free to validate; go Pro ($25/mo) at launch for daily backups and no pausing.
nodes.norm_label: option c: stay on managed supabase

nodes.label: Option A: Cloudflare Full Backend (Workers + D1 + R2 + Pages)
nodes.rationale: Rejected for this project: D1 is SQLite not Postgres (rewrite all 19 migrations, enums, triggers), no Row-Level Security (re-implement every rule imperatively in Workers), no consumer auth, weeks of re-platforming a working backend. 'Great platform, wrong time.'
nodes.norm_label: option a: cloudflare full backend (workers + d1 + r2 + pages)

nodes.label: Option B: Self-Host Supabase on Hostinger VPS
nodes.rationale: Rejected: trades a $0–25/month managed bill for $4,800–9,600 of hidden first-year DevOps time; you become the DBA, backup operator, and security patcher — a distraction and availability risk for an MVP chasing product-market fit. Only rational for data-residency needs with DevOps skills to spare.
nodes.norm_label: option b: self-host supabase on hostinger vps

nodes.label: Recommended Hybrid: Supabase + Cloudflare Pages + Worker/Edge Fn
nodes.rationale: Keep managed Supabase, use Cloudflare where it wins (free Pages frontend, zero-egress R2 for images later), don't self-host, don't re-platform. Preserves working Postgres + RLS + Auth, closes the audit's security gaps with a small trusted function instead of a rewrite, keeps monthly cost $0–25 while time stays on product. Cheapest sticker price (full Cloudflare / self-host) is most expensive in engineering time and risk.
nodes.norm_label: recommended hybrid: supabase + cloudflare pages + worker/edge fn

nodes.label: Trusted Server Tier (Supabase Edge Function or Cloudflare Worker)
nodes.rationale: The single highest-value change requiring no vendor switch: compute total_price server-side, write payment_status only from the payment webhook, gate reviews on completed bookings — fixing the audit's critical findings.
nodes.norm_label: trusted server tier (supabase edge function or cloudflare worker)

nodes.label: Changelog: Server-Side Price Fetching in Booking Flow
nodes.rationale: Removed client-side price passing in the booking flow to prevent manipulation; useCreateBooking fetches price securely; hardcoded/fake ratings and mock data removed for data accuracy.
nodes.norm_label: changelog: server-side price fetching in booking flow

nodes.label: Arabic-First Localization Audit & 6-Stage Execution Plan
nodes.rationale: All 6 stages landed on feat/arabic-first (2026-07-04). Verdict: project genuinely RTL-aware already (logical:physical CSS 629:30); gaps systematic but bounded. Each stage is one commit, independently shippable and revertible; no DB/schema/API changes in the plan.

nodes.label: Centralized Formatters (formatDate/formatCurrency/formatNumber/formatRelativeTime)
nodes.rationale: Every module reinvented formatting and date-fns format() never received the ar locale — the biggest silent Arabic-UX failure. One src/lib/formatters.ts reading i18n.language fixes the class.

nodes.label: Numeral Policy: Western Digits with Arabic Locale Formatting
nodes.rationale: ar-SA Intl produces Arabic-Indic digits (٤٩) by default, inconsistent with the Western digits used throughout ar.json; recommendation is 'ar' with -u-nu-latn, matching current convention and regional fintech norms.
nodes.norm_label: numeral policy: western digits with arabic locale formatting

nodes.label: Arabic CLDR Plural Forms Support
nodes.rationale: Arabic has 6 CLDR plural forms and the JSON had none, so count-based strings render grammatically wrong for most counts; i18next supports CLDR Arabic plurals natively via _zero/_one/_two/_few/_many/_other keys.

nodes.label: RTL Functional Fixes (icons, carousel keyboard, aligns, sidebar)
nodes.norm_label: rtl functional fixes (icons, carousel keyboard, aligns, sidebar)

nodes.label: Localized SEO Layer (Seo component, hreflang, JSON-LD, sitemap)
nodes.rationale: Structural ceiling acknowledged: client-rendered SPA means crawlers see an empty shell; prerendering/SSR documented as a separate future project. hreflang via ?lng= query param chosen for zero routing change over /ar/ path prefix.
nodes.norm_label: localized seo layer (seo component, hreflang, json-ld, sitemap)

nodes.label: Platform-Configurable Currency (VITE_PLATFORM_CURRENCY: USD/SAR/AED)
nodes.rationale: Original recommendation was locale-aware USD only (multi-currency is a product feature, not localization); implemented as a single configurable platform currency env var covering USD/SAR/AED.

nodes.label: ToTravel Multi-Tenant Tour Marketplace
nodes.rationale: Implementation-driven as-is PRD: documents the actual implemented state, not aspirational features — features marked Partial/Stubbed have code present but are not fully functional.

nodes.label: Traveler / Agency / Admin Role Model
nodes.rationale: Enforcement is three-layered: ProtectedRoute at UI level, RLS policies at API level, foreign keys at DB level. Admin enforcement noted as relying primarily on UI-level checks.

nodes.label: Payment Integration Missing (schema exists, no gateway)
nodes.norm_label: payment integration missing (schema exists, no gateway)

nodes.label: Messaging System Stub (UI only, no messages table)
nodes.norm_label: messaging system stub (ui only, no messages table)

nodes.label: VITE_* Environment Variable Contract
nodes.rationale: All client config uses Vite VITE_ prefix and is inlined into the browser bundle at build time — publishable keys only, never secrets; production values come from the host's environment.

nodes.label: robots.txt Crawler Rules + Sitemap Pointer
nodes.rationale: Allows all crawlers; sitemap URL uses a placeholder domain (totravel.example.com) that must be replaced before deploying.

hyperedges.label: Server-Side Trust Boundary for Pricing & Payments
